description: Forked gpu-screen-recorder plugin with GSR-style multi-track audio and additional encoder options

messages.no-portals-desc: Start xdg-desktop-portal and a compositor portal (wlr/hyprland/gnome/kde).
messages.not-installed-desc: Please install gpu-screen-recorder to use screen recording features.
messages.monitor-not-found: Could not detect the monitor under cursor.

name: GSR-Noctalia

audio.tracks-desc: Each card below is one audio track in the recorded file — tracks stay separate so a video editor can mute/remix them later. A track can have an Output slot (desktop / app audio), an Input slot (mic), or both (they get pre-mixed into that single track). Click "+ Add audio track" to create more tracks. Click "Custom expression…" on a track only if you need advanced gpu-screen-recorder syntax like app-inverse:firefox.
audio.no-tracks: No audio tracks configured — recording will be silent.
audio.codec-desc: Opus is recommended for best performance and smallest audio size
audio.bitrate-desc: Leave empty for default (128 for opus/flac, 160 for aac). Set 0 for automatic.
audio.title: Audio Settings

filename-pattern.description: Filename pattern using date/time codes (e.g., recording_yyyyMMdd_HHmmss)
filename-pattern.label: Filename pattern

general.copy-to-clipboard-description: Copy the file to clipboard after recording finishes.
general.hide-when-inactive-description: Hide the bar indicator when not recording
general.output-folder-description: Folder where screen recordings will be saved
general.restore-portal-session-description: Automatically restore the previous portal session for subsequent recordings (disables the selection prompt)
general.show-cursor-description: Record mouse cursor in the video
general.container-desc: Video container (file extension). mp4 is most compatible; mkv is best for long recordings.
general.post-save-script-desc: Script to run after each save. Receives (filepath, type) where type is 'regular', 'replay', or 'screenshot'.
general.title: General

replay.enable-desc: Keep a rolling buffer of recent screen activity that can be saved on demand
replay.duration-desc: Length of the replay buffer in seconds
replay.custom-duration-desc: Enter a custom replay buffer duration in seconds
replay.storage-desc: Where to store the replay buffer
replay.restart-on-save-desc: Restart the replay buffer each time a replay is saved (-restart-replay-on-save)

video.codec-desc: h264 is the most common codec; 10-bit variants reduce banding
video.color-range-desc: Limited is recommended for better compatibility
video.frame-rate-desc: Target frame rate for screen recordings
video.custom-frame-rate-desc: Enter a custom frame rate value (FPS)
video.frame-rate-mode-desc: vfr is default. content only works with X11 or portal and syncs to screen updates.
video.quality-desc: Higher quality results in larger file sizes
video.bitrate-mode-desc: auto→qp. QP keeps constant quality; VBR/CBR keep constant size. CBR recommended for replay/streaming.
video.resolution-desc: Limit output resolution. Default uses original screen resolution
video.source-desc: Portal is recommended, if you get artifacts try Screen
video.encoder-desc: GPU is default. CPU encoding only supports h264.
video.fallback-cpu-desc: If GPU encoding fails at start, fall back to CPU.
video.keyint-desc: Leave empty for default (2.0). Smaller values improve seek precision; larger reduces file size.
video.title: Video Settings
